sections.paragraph:
καταπονέω aor. 3 pl. κατεπόνησαν (TestLevi 6:9) (s. πονέω; Hippocr.; Polyb., Diod S 11, 6, 3; pap, LXX; TestLevi 6:9; Joseph.) to cause distress through oppressive means, subdue, torment, wear out, oppress τινά someone B 20:2b; D 5:2b. In our lit. predom. in pres. pass. ptc. καταπονούμενος (Aelian, VH 3, 27 ὑπὸ πενίας καταπονούμενος; Jos., Bell. 2, 313, Ant. 7, 124) Ac 4:2 D. Of Lot 2 Pt 2:7.—Subst. ὁ καταπονούμενος the one who is oppressed, mistreated, weary (UPZ 110, 88 [164 b.c.]) ποιεῖν ἐκδίκησιν τῷ κ. see to it that justice is done for the oppressed Ac 7:24; πονεῖν ἐπὶ κ. toil for one who is downtrodden B 20:2a; D 5:2a.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

pres. pres. = present

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 525
